title: bredemeyera microphylla
intro: Bredemeyera microphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the 'yellow jessie' or 'yellow ironwood', native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bredemeyera microphylla include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Bredemeyera microphylla can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal treatment should be further researched and confirmed by medical professionals.
